pattern: Splice list2 into list1 by rewiring the node just before index a directly to list2's head and list2's tail directly to the node just after index b, discarding everything between.
hints: You don't need to build a new list from scratch — removing a range and inserting another list is just a matter of relinking a few pointers.
Walk list1 to find the node right before index a and the node right after index b; those are the only two anchors on list1 you actually need to keep.
Point the node before a at list2's head, walk list2 to its own tail, then point that tail at the node after b; return list1's original head.

edge_inputs.description: a equals b removes a single node and splices list2 in its place
removed range covers everything except the two end nodes
minimal list1 length with a single interior node replaced
a short removed segment replaced by a single-node list2
single node removed near the tail, replaced by a longer list2
zero values mixed with large values on both sides of the splice
larger list1 and list2 to exercise pointer walking past several nodes

stress.description: randomized list1/list2 lengths with a always <= b within bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n1 = rng.randint(3, max(3, min(size, 2000)))
    vals1 = [rng.randint(0, 1000000) for _ in range(n1)]
    a = rng.randint(1, n1 - 2)
    b = rng.randint(a, n1 - 2)
    n2 = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 2000)))
    vals2 = [rng.randint(0, 1000000) for _ in range(n2)]
    return [vals1, a, b, vals2]
stress.note: n1/n2 scale with size but stay well under the 1e4 constraint cap for harness speed

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def mergeInBetween(self, list1, a, b, list2):
        vals1 = []
        node = list1
        while node:
            vals1.append(node.val)
            node = node.next
        vals2 = []
        node = list2
        while node:
            vals2.append(node.val)
            node = node.next
        merged = vals1[:a] + vals2 + vals1[b + 1:]
        head = None
        for v in reversed(merged):
            head = ListNode(v, head)
        return head
